dispensation.title: Dispensation
dispensation.intro: This remedy is dispensed from the cabinet and not presented as a retail commodity. The amount below covers preparation, packaging, and delivery, and is provided after the monograph so the evidence may be read first
dispensation.unavailable: This remedy is currently unavailable. Write to us to receive a return notice when stock is restored
dispensation.note: There is no need to decide immediately. Read the full monograph before requesting dispensation

monographExtras.evidenceIntro: Established evidence and emerging findings are listed separately. Neither replaces Prophetic grading or traditional scholarly standing

monograph.suggestedUseIntro: Offered as a traditional dietary means and not as treatment for a diagnosed medical condition
monograph.pullQuoteDocumentation: Every remedy is documented to source. We state what we know, name what we do not, and dispense means with clear limits
monograph.photoBlackSeed: A scholar's research desk with botanical journal on Nigella sativa, pressed specimens, and black seeds
monograph.photoHoney: Golden honey being poured from a wooden dipper into a glass vessel, beside dried thyme
monograph.photoOliveOil: Premium olive oil in a glass cruet beside ripe olives and olive leaves on weathered wood
monograph.photoSenna: Dried senna pods and leaves arranged on ivory linen beside a ceramic apothecary jar

nav.label: Duʿa & Dhikr
nav.description: Morning and evening remembrance collections from the Sunnah.

landing.heading: Daily Dhikr
landing.lede: Verified daily remembrance from the Sunnah, published under the Knowledge Library's editorial standard.
landing.aboutBody1: Daily Dhikr is a resource of verified daily remembrance from the Sunnah, presented under the same editorial standard that governs the rest of the Knowledge Library.
landing.aboutBody2: Every entry passes both a scholarly review and an editorial review before it is published. Nothing appears here on the strength of common usage alone.
landing.languageBody1: The Arabic text is the authoritative source for every entry. English and Danish translations are derived from the Arabic and checked against it, not published as independent content.
landing.languageBody2: No account, sign-in, or download is required to read what is published here.

emptyState.heading: Preparing for publication
emptyState.body: Daily Dhikr is being prepared and reviewed. Verified entries will appear here once scholarly and editorial review is complete.

timeNavigation.supporting: Choose the remembrance collection for the beginning or close of your day

dhikrMorning.heading: Morning Dhikr
dhikrMorning.lede: Remembrance recited at the start of the day, prepared from documented sources.

emptyState.heading: Under scholarly review
emptyState.body: Morning Dhikr is currently undergoing scholarly review and will be available soon.

errorState.heading: Temporarily unavailable
errorState.body: Morning Dhikr could not be loaded right now. Please try again shortly.

dhikrMorning.editorialNotice: Editorial review status: These entries have been prepared from documented sources and are awaiting independent scholarly verification. They should not be treated as a formal fatwa or definitive hadith-authentication ruling.
dhikrMorning.collectionIntroBody: This collection contains 30 entries from the Sunnah Remedies source register. Two entries have completed editorial review and are published pending independent scholarly verification. The remaining entries are provided as a clearly marked reference collection while their wording, translation and sourcing continue to be reviewed.
dhikrMorning.sectionEditoriallyReviewedDescription: Prepared from documented sources by an editorial reviewer and awaiting independent scholarly verification.
dhikrMorning.sectionReferenceHeading: Reference collection — scholarly and editorial review pending
dhikrMorning.referenceCollectionCount: {count, plural, one {1 entry awaiting review} other {# entries awaiting review}}
dhikrMorning.referenceCollectionNotice: The entries below reproduce the source collection for reference and memorisation support. Their wording, translation, attribution, timing, repetition count, and virtue statements may still require verification. They are not presented as confirmed authentic rulings or final scholarly conclusions.
dhikrMorning.pendingRepetitionLabel: Source document records: {count, plural, one {# repetition} other {# repetitions}} — verification pending

dhikrEvening.heading: Evening Dhikr
dhikrEvening.reviewStatusNotice: The Evening Dhikr collection has not yet completed editorial or scholarly review. Nothing on this page should be read as verified or approved remembrance

emptyState.heading: Collection in preparation
emptyState.body: The Evening Dhikr collection is currently being prepared and reviewed. It will be published here once the wording, sourcing and translations are ready

errorState.heading: Temporarily unavailable
errorState.body: Evening Dhikr could not be loaded right now. Please try again shortly.

dhikrEvening.editorialNotice: Editorial review status: These entries have been prepared from documented sources and are awaiting independent scholarly verification. They should not be treated as a formal fatwa or definitive hadith-authentication ruling.
dhikrEvening.collectionIntroBody: Close the day with remembrances documented for the evening. Reviewed entries are presented separately from the wider reference collection so that their current editorial status remains clear.
dhikrEvening.sectionEditoriallyReviewedDescription: Prepared from documented sources by an editorial reviewer and awaiting independent scholarly verification.
dhikrEvening.sectionReferenceHeading: Reference collection — scholarly and editorial review pending
dhikrEvening.referenceCollectionCount: {count, plural, one {1 entry awaiting review} other {# entries awaiting review}}
dhikrEvening.referenceCollectionNotice: The entries below reproduce the source collection for reference and memorisation support. Their wording, translation, attribution, timing, repetition count, and virtue statements may still require verification. They are not presented as confirmed authentic rulings or final scholarly conclusions.
dhikrEvening.pendingRepetitionLabel: Source document records: {count, plural, one {# repetition} other {# repetitions}} — verification pending
dhikrEvening.methodologyBody: Entries reach this page through one of two pathways: full scholarly and editorial approval, or editorial-only publication pending independent scholarly verification. Records that have not yet completed either pathway appear in the reference collection below, clearly marked as pending, with their protected Arabic wording shown exactly as transcribed from the source document.

booking.refreshNote: Your selections are kept in this browser session while you move between steps. Refreshing restores them here — they are never placed in the page address. Optional health notes are not stored across refresh

homepage.footerColophon: Sunnah Remedies · Institute of Prophetic Medicine
homepage.eyebrow: EST. MMXXV · INSTITUTE OF PROPHETIC MEDICINE
homepage.arrivalEnglish: The medicine of the prophetic tradition, kept living
homepage.standfirst: An institution for the preservation and responsible transmission of prophetic medicine — grounded in primary-source scholarship, laboratory verification, clinical accountability, and plain limits
homepage.trustLine: Primary-source scholarship, laboratory verification, clinical accountability, and plain limits — before commerce

latestAdditions.heading: New from Sunnah Remedies
latestAdditions.lede: Recently published resources, programmes and guidance from across the institution

journeys.label: I am considering a Sacred Journey
journeys.description: See educational pilgrimage with preparation before departure

foundingCovenant.heading: The principles behind everything we do
foundingCovenant.intro0: Sunnah Remedies was not established simply to provide treatments, sell products or deliver courses
foundingCovenant.intro1: It was established to preserve and responsibly transmit the tradition of Prophetic Medicine through scholarship, clinical care, professional education and carefully selected natural therapeutics
foundingCovenant.introLead: Our work is governed by a founding covenant:
foundingCovenant.mission: To make authentic Prophetic Medicine accessible through responsible scholarship, compassionate clinical care, professional education and carefully governed natural therapeutics — delivered with integrity, clarity and respect for every individual
foundingCovenant.vision: To become a trusted international institution for Prophetic Medicine: preserving its authentic tradition, raising standards of practice and building an enduring body of clinical care, education and knowledge that can serve generations

healing.title: Healing belongs to Allah
healing.body: We recognise that healing is from Allah alone. Treatments, practitioners and remedies are means and are never presented as guarantees

foundation.title: Nothing without a traceable foundation
foundation.body: Claims concerning the Qur’an, Sunnah or Prophetic Medicine must be supported by identifiable and responsibly presented sources

fear.title: We do not trade in fear
fear.body: We will never exaggerate illness, exploit vulnerability or pressure people into purchasing treatments, products or courses

person.title: The person comes before the protocol
person.body: Every individual is treated with dignity, listened to carefully and supported according to their circumstances — never reduced to a transaction

trust.title: Trust before growth
trust.body: We will not compromise safety, authenticity or professional standards in pursuit of faster expansion or greater commercial success

foundingCovenant.closing: This is not simply a statement of intent. It is the standard by which our institution asks to be judged

homepage.traditionStandfirst: What is this discipline, and why does it require an institution?
homepage.traditionBody0: Prophetic Medicine is not folk remedy, not alternative wellness, not a brand opportunity. It is a received body of knowledge — transmitted through authenticated chains, tested against observable evidence, and constrained by the limits its own sources declare
homepage.traditionBody1: This institution exists because the discipline deserves the same rigour as any established medical tradition: verifiable scholarship, clinical governance, transparent provenance, and patient dignity at every encounter
homepage.pullQuoteText: Healing is from Allah; the remedy is a means
homepage.thresholdPurpose: The threshold — crossing into the institution
homepage.thresholdComposition: Wide establishing shot, strong architecture, human presence suggested not centred
homepage.thresholdAlt: Scholarly hands examining an illuminated manuscript of Prophetic medicine beside glass vessels of amber oil and black seed
homepage.thresholdQualifier: Received scholarship, clinical practice, and transparent provenance
homepage.correspondenceBody: Occasional correspondence on scholarship, new monographs, and programme announcements. No frequency promise; no promotional content
homepage.correspondenceConsent: You will receive institutional correspondence by email. Unsubscribe at any time by replying or using the link provided
homepage.institutionStatement: Knowledge before commerce. Service before profit. Trust before growth. The institution exists for the next generation
homepage.deptKnowledgeStandfirst: The open shelf — monographs, research notes, and patient guides, all graded and cited
homepage.deptKnowledgePlateAlt: A scholarly reading room with open manuscripts and natural light
homepage.deptAcademyStandfirst: Clinical education in Hijama and Prophetic therapeutics, structured by isnād
homepage.deptAcademyPlatePurpose: Tutorial room with faculty and students
homepage.deptAcademyPlateAlt: Students in a scholarly classroom studying anatomical charts and medical texts under natural light
homepage.deptApothecaryStandfirst: A cabinet of preparations, each documented to source before dispensation
homepage.deptApothecaryPlateAlt: An apothecary dispensary with amber glass vessels of honey and oils arranged on aged wooden shelving
homepage.deptJourneysStandfirst: Educational pilgrimage to the Holy Lands - purpose, preparation & departure
homepage.deptJourneysPlatePurpose: Masjid an-Nabawi at golden hour — quiet courtyard and green dome
homepage.deptJourneysPlateAlt: Masjid an-Nabawi at golden hour — warm marble courtyard, green dome, and distant pilgrims in quiet reflection
